_comment: REAL probes file for Mama's Munches (gate-1) — Spec 36 §8 / FR-36-16. Authored 2026-07-20 against the LIVE sandybrown canary DOM, not from the block source: every selector below was confirmed present by querying the rendered page (5 bar items Shop/Our Story/Send to Ward/Gift Ideas/FAQs; drawer = <dialog class='sgs-nav-drawer'> containing its own .sgs-nav-menu__bar). Baseline target per Spec 36 §8: 10/10 at 375.
_selector_note: There are TWO .sgs-nav-menu__bar instances on the page — the header bar (.sgs-site-header > … > .sgs-container__inner) and the drawer's copy (.sgs-nav-drawer__body). Every probe below is scoped to one or the other; NEVER use a bare .sgs-nav-menu__bar, which matches both and silently probes whichever comes first in the DOM.
_viewport_note: elementfrompoint-sweep.mjs opens every viewport at height 1200, so yRatio is a fraction of 1200 — not of a real device height. The 0.028 header-row ratio = the ~34px close-control centre measured live.

375._comment: Mobile: burger opens the full-screen <dialog> drawer. 10 probes = the Spec 36 §8 Mama's baseline. Proves (a) the header row hit-tests to the close control, (b) every drawer link is reachable at its own centre, (c) everything below the header is unreachable — the modal owns the hit-test.

768._comment: Tablet: collapsePoint is 768, so the BAR is shown (no burger, no drawer) — confirmed live. No openSelector: there is nothing to open. These probes prove nothing overlays the bar and steals its hit-test (sticky rows, the cart icon, a stray full-width container).

1440._comment: Desktop: flat bar. Phase 1 ships NO mega menu (FR-36-5 is a later phase), so unlike probes.example.json there is no mega trigger to open and no mega-scrim to probe — do not add one until the mega actually ships, or the sweep will exit 2 on a selector matching 0 elements.
